steps.title: Connect zeros to the graph
steps.content: The equation $f(x)=0$ is true at exactly the points where the graph of $y=f(x)$ intersects the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Count the intersections
steps.content: From the graph, the curve does not intersect the $x$-axis. Therefore, there are $0$ values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. The answer is D.
